title: Commentary on Investments: As of March 31, 2026
content:
Total investments declined to $40,517 thousand as of March 31, 2026, reflecting a decrease of 10.9% from the previous period. The portfolio remains structured as shown and continues to be heavily weighted toward held-to-maturity debt securities, which total $40,408 thousand and represent virtually the entire investment portfolio.

Other investments totaled $110 thousand and remain immaterial relative to the overall portfolio. Held-to-maturity debt securities increased by 16.5% from the previous quarter, strengthening liquidity or earning asset base as appropriate for a credit union's balance sheet needs.
The investment maturity structure remains well distributed across multiple time horizons, supporting effective liquidity management and interest rate risk control. The mix of maturity buckets provides flexibility to respond to changing market conditions while maintaining alignment with policy guidelines.
Overall, the credit union’s investment portfolio remains conservative, liquid, and aligned with policy guidelines. Management continues to emphasize capital preservation, stable yield generation, and prudent duration management while maintaining flexibility to respond to changing balance-sheet needs and market opportunities.
